upstream_tag_rationale: Earl Grey is the OpenTitan top the TEE plan (docs/security/tee-plan/02-root-of-trust.md S1) names. The silver_release_v5 tag is a stable, externally referenceable Earl Grey checkpoint; we pin its resolved commit so check_rot_integration.py can enforce HEAD==pin. Bump decisions are recorded in audit_notes.
fork_policy_reason: OpenTitan is audited, taped-out, Apache-2.0 RTL. Per docs/security/tee-plan/02-root-of-trust.md S1 Option A we integrate it via a wrapper (rtl/security/rot/e1_rot_top.sv), never fork.
integration_note: otp_ctrl and lc_ctrl are NOT reused from OpenTitan in E1: they are replaced by the E1-specific W4 rtl/security/otp/e1_otp_map.sv and W5 rtl/security/lc/e1_lc_ctrl.sv per docs/security/tee-plan/02-root-of-trust.md S4. The remaining crypto/security blocks are the OpenTitan reuse target for the RoT wrapper.
audit_notes: Pinned at bootstrap-resolution time to earlgrey_silver_release_v5 (20a5166). The OpenTitan crypto/security blocks listed in reused_blocks require topgen/fusesoc-generated reg packages (*_reg_pkg.sv, *_reg_top.sv) plus the full prim dependency chain to elaborate; until those generated files are staged into the checkout, the RoT wrapper binds them via fail-closed E1 integration shims with the correct TL-UL signature, and check_rot_integration.py reports each as BLOCKED with the named missing dependency. The Ibex RoT core elaborates for real from the FuseSoC-staged tree under external/ibex/ibex/build (shared with the PMC lane).
